justifications.ip1: All three ACs have distinct, specific failing test scenarios: T1 asserts 'positive pacs.002 when processing completes in 100ms' (AC1), T2 asserts 'SCHEME_SLA_EXCEEDED before 10,000ms when processing stalls past 9,500ms' (AC2), and T3/T4 assert receipt timestamp, ack timestamp, and elapsedMs present in every log entry (AC3).
justifications.ip2: No tasks implement any item from the 'What will NOT be built' list (fraud vendor API, outbound RTP, AML load testing, batch reconciliation); C3's hard 9,500ms threshold is reflected as a named constant `SLA_THRESHOLD_MS = 9500` in the implementation task with a checklist note 'C3 (HARD scheme rule implementation)'.
justifications.ip3: Every task names specific files (`tests/payments/rtp-sla-timer.test.js`, `src/payments/rtp-sla-timer.js`, `src/payments/rtp-message-handler.js`, `tests/payments/rtp-sla-timer.performance.test.js`) and provides concrete code with explicit assertions such as `expect(ackCall.args[0]).to.deep.include({ type: 'pacs.002', status: 'REJECTED', reason: 'SCHEME_SLA_EXCEEDED' })`.
justifications.ip4: Test-first order is preserved throughout — Task 1 writes the full failing test suite before Task 2 writes any implementation — but the plan does not include an explicit step to run the tests and confirm RED state before proceeding to Task 2.
justifications.ip5: NFR-1 is addressed by a dedicated Task 5 (`T_NFR_1`) that asserts `expect(p99).to.be.lessThan(9000)` across 200 messages at 40 tps; C3's compiled-in-constant is correctly implemented as `const SLA_THRESHOLD_MS = 9500` (not a configurable parameter).

notes: Exceptionally thorough plan with complete AC coverage, correct hard-constant enforcement for C3, and a dedicated T_NFR_1 performance test; minor deduction only for omitting an explicit 'run tests → confirm RED' execution step between Task 1 and Task 2.
